estimation_meta.method: Theory only. Tight ST=O(nw) tradeoff proven. No implementation.

footnotes: Theory only. First single-server client preprocessing PIR achieving tight space-time tradeoff ST=O(nw) for w=Omega(log n). Deterministic correctness. No implementation.

group_specific.assumption: Harmony-AES: OWF (AES PRF). Harmony-FF1: empirical FPE security (FF1, NIST-standardized)
group_specific.space_time_tradeoff: ST = O(nw) (tight, matches Yeo lower bound)

asymptotic.response_size: Additive HE: O(d*n^{1/d} + F^{d-1}); SHE (MulPIR): O(d*n^{1/d}); GR: O(lambda_GR)
asymptotic.server_computation: Additive HE: O(N*(A+S)); SHE: O(N*(A+S) + N^{(d-1)/d}*M); GR: O(2*n*pt mult of lambda_GR-bit numbers)

estimation_meta.method: Approximate values read from Figure 9 (log-scale bar chart). 4 GB Twitter DB, client storage capped at 200 MB, kappa_avg=0.8, kappa_worst=0.01, batch size B=24.

footnotes: Figure 9 reports ~195 queries/sec at B=24 for distributional PIR vs ~2 queries/sec for no-batching baseline (SimplePIR). Exact absolute throughput/communication values not tabulated; only relative improvements (10-195x queries/sec, 4.8-9.7x less communication vs no batching).

CrowdSurf end-to-end: PIR GPU time 4 ms + hint compression 540 ms CPU = 544 ms total server time. Communication download 21 MB. Server uses GPU (V100) for PIR, CPU cluster for hint compression. B=24 batch size, kappa_avg=0.8, kappa_worst=0.01.
server_time_ms=544 is total (GPU PIR 4 ms + CPU hint compression 540 ms). Cost $0.0057/request.

estimation_meta.method: Approximate values read from Figure 10 (log-scale bar chart). 4 GB DB subset, kappa_avg=0.8, kappa_worst=0.01.

footnotes: Figure 10 reports ~3.5 queries/sec at B=64 for distributional PIR (Respire) vs ~0.3 queries/sec for no-batching. 6.7-12.8x more queries/sec, 2.3-117x less communication vs no batching.
SCT auditing application: server CPU 91 core-ms, communication 561 KB, client storage 6 MB. Underlying PIR is YPIR. Compared to plain YPIR: 12x less server CPU, 3x less communication.
